title: Chimichanga

ingredients.title: For the Filling:
items: 1 cup sautéed onions and bell peppers (mixed colors)
Salt and black pepper to taste
1/2 cup shredded cheddar or Monterey Jack cheese
1/4 cup chicken or beef broth (optional, for moisture)

ingredients.title: For Garnish / Serving:
items: Pico de gallo or fresh salsa

instruction.title: Prepare the Filling
steps: Heat a skillet over medium heat and sauté onions, bell peppers, and garlic until softened (about 5 minutes).
Add shredded chicken (or beef/pork) and spices: cumin, smoked paprika, chili powder, salt, and pepper. Stir to combine.
Add broth if needed to keep it moist. Cook until heated through, then stir in 1/2 cup cheese and cilantro. Remove from heat.

instruction.title: Assemble the Chimichangas
steps: Warm tortillas slightly to make them pliable.
Place 1/2 cup of filling in the center of each tortilla. Sprinkle a little extra cheese on top.
Fold the sides in, then roll tightly from one end to the other, ensuring the filling is sealed inside.

instruction.title: Fry to Perfection
steps: Heat oil in a deep skillet or Dutch oven to 350°F (175°C).
Carefully place the rolled chimichangas seam side down into the hot oil. Fry 2–3 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy.
Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.

instruction.title: Serve Like a Top Restaurant
steps: Place chimichangas on a platter, garnish with cilantro, lime wedges, and serve with sides: guacamole, sour cream, and pico de gallo.
Optional: drizzle with a rich enchilada sauce or melted cheese for extra indulgence.

chefTips: For extra flavor, marinate the chicken in lime juice, garlic, and spices before cooking.
To reduce oil absorption, lightly brush with oil and bake at 400°F for 15–20 minutes instead of deep-frying.
Use high-quality tortillas; fresh, large flour tortillas hold the filling better.
